channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks
item.description: A Chinese threat actor exploited the video conferencing platform to perform reconnaissance, escalate privileges, and execute additional payloads. The post TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Symantec vulnerability, anti-ClickFix mechanism added to macOS, FBI hack classified as major incident. The post In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical ShareFile Flaws Lead to Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: The vulnerabilities can be chained together to bypass authentication and upload arbitrary files to the server. The post Critical ShareFile Flaws Lead to Unauthenticated RCE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: UNC1069 Social Engineering of Axios Maintainer Led to npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The maintainer of the Axios npm package has confirmed that the supply chain compromise was the result of a highly-targeted social engineering campaign orchestrated by North Korean threat actors tracked as UNC1069. Maintainer Jason Saayman said the attackers tailored their social...

item.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture
item.description: The next major breach hitting your clients probably won't come from inside their walls. It'll come through a vendor they trust, a SaaS tool their finance team signed up for, or a subcontractor nobody in IT knows about. That's the new attack surface, and most organizations are...

item.title: Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control
item.description: Shadow AI embedded in everyday apps, combined with outdated mobile devices and zero-click exploits, is creating a new and largely unseen mobile risk. The post Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign
item.description: Using automated scanning and the Nexus Listener collection framework, the hackers compromised over 750 systems. The post React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Genspark Raises $385 Million, Accelerates Commercialization of AI Agent Platform
item.description: Genspark has raised $385 million at a $1.6 billion valuation, as its AI Agent platform gains traction in Japan with strong user engagement. The company is scaling its “AI Workspace” to accelerate enterprise adoption.

item.title: Mizzen Insight Raises Nearly $10 Million as AI User Research Platform Scales Rapidly
item.description: Mizzen Insight raised nearly $10 million to scale its AI-powered user research platform, cutting research cycles from weeks to under a day while serving clients like AlibabaGroup and Xiaomi.

item.title: EVE Energy Reports 2025 Revenue Growth as Multi-Segment Strategy Supports Expansion
item.description: EVE Energy reported $8.5 billion in 2025 revenue, driven by strong growth in both power and energy storage batteries, supported by global expansion and diversified operations.

item.title: LONGi Launches Energy Storage Strategy, Targets Overseas Markets and System Integration
item.description: LONGiSolar launched its “LONGi ONE” strategy to expand into energy storage, focusing on system integration and overseas markets with expected returns by 2027–2028.

item.title: New SparkCat Variant in iOS, Android Apps Steals Crypto Wallet Recovery Phrase Images
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new version of the SparkCat malware on the Apple App Store and Google Play Store, more than a year after the trojan was discovered targeting both the mobile operating systems. The malware has been found to conceal itself...

item.title: UBTECH Offers Up to $17 Million Salary to Hire Chief Scientist as Humanoid Robot Revenue Grows
item.description: UBTECHRobotics is offering up to $17 million annually to hire a chief scientist, as humanoid robots become its largest revenue driver with over 1,000 units sold in 2025.

item.title: WARP connectivity issues affecting a subset of windows users, using latest version 2026.3.846.0.
item.description: Apr 3, 08:45 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.Apr 3, 08:45 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented, and we are currently monitoring.Apr 3, 08:39 UTCUpdate - We are continuing the investigation of this issueApr 3, 08:08 UTCInvestigating - We are currently...

item.title: Drift Loses $285 Million in Durable Nonce Social Engineering Attack Linked to DPRK
item.description: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1, 2026. "Earlier today, a malicious actor gained unauthorized access to Drift Protocol through a novel attack involving...

item.title: [AINews] Gemma 4: The best small Multimodal Open Models, dramatically better than Gemma 3 in every way
item.description: A welcome update from Google!

item.title: Self-Routing: Parameter-Free Expert Routing from Hidden States
item.description: arXiv:2604.00421v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) layers increase model capacity by activating only a small subset of experts per token, and typically rely on a learned router to map hidden states to expert assignments. In this work, we ask whether a dedicated learned router...

item.title: Execution-Verified Reinforcement Learning for Optimization Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2604.00442v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automating optimization modeling with LLMs is a promising path toward scalable decision intelligence, but existing approaches either rely on agentic pipelines built on closed-source LLMs with high inference latency, or fine-tune smaller LLMs using...

item.title: Towards Reliable Truth-Aligned Uncertainty Estimation in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.00445v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Uncertainty estimation (UE) aims to detect hallucinated outputs of large language models (LLMs) to improve their reliability. However, UE metrics often exhibit unstable performance across configurations, which significantly limits their applicability....

item.title: Logarithmic Scores, Power-Law Discoveries: Disentangling Measurement from Coverage in Agent-Based Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00477v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based agent judges are an emerging approach to evaluating conversational AI, yet a fundamental uncertainty remains: can we trust their assessments, and if so, how many are needed? Through 960 sessions with two model pairs across 15 tasks, we show...

item.title: The Silicon Mirror: Dynamic Behavioral Gating for Anti-Sycophancy in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.00478v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly prioritize user validation over epistemic accuracy - a phenomenon known as sycophancy. We present The Silicon Mirror, an orchestration framework that dynamically detects user persuasion tactics and adjusts AI...

item.title: DySCo: Dynamic Semantic Compression for Effective Long-term Time Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2604.01261v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series forecasting (TSF) is critical across domains such as finance, meteorology, and energy. While extending the lookback window theoretically provides richer historical context, in practice, it often introduces irrelevant noise and...

item.title: Sven: Singular Value Descent as a Computationally Efficient Natural Gradient Method
item.description: arXiv:2604.01279v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Sven (Singular Value dEsceNt), a new optimization algorithm for neural networks that exploits the natural decomposition of loss functions into a sum over individual data points, rather than reducing the full loss to a single scalar before...

item.title: Forecasting Supply Chain Disruptions with Foresight Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.01298v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Anticipating supply chain disruptions before they materialize is a core challenge for firms and policymakers alike. A key difficulty is learning to reason reliably about infrequent, high-impact events from noisy and unstructured inputs - a setting...

item.title: UQ-SHRED: uncertainty quantification of shallow recurrent decoder networks for sparse sensing via engression
item.description: arXiv:2604.01305v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reconstructing high-dimensional spatiotemporal fields from sparse sensor measurements is critical in a wide range of scientific applications. The SHallow REcurrent Decoder (SHRED) architecture is a recent state-of-the-art architecture that...

item.title: An Online Machine Learning Multi-resolution Optimization Framework for Energy System Design Limit of Performance Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.01308v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Designing reliable integrated energy systems for industrial processes requires optimization and verification models across multiple fidelities, from architecture-level sizing to high-fidelity dynamic operation. However, model mismatch across...

item.title: How Trustworthy Are LLM-as-Judge Ratings for Interpretive Responses? Implications for Qualitative Research Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2604.00008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As qualitative researchers show growing interest in using automated tools to support interpretive analysis, a large language model (LLM) is often introduced into an analytic workflow as is, without systematic evaluation of interpretive quality or...

item.title: Eyla: Toward an Identity-Anchored LLM Architecture with Integrated Biological Priors -- Vision, Implementation Attempt, and Lessons from AI-Assisted Development
item.description: arXiv:2604.00009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the design rationale, implementation attempt, and failure analysis of Eyla, a proposed identity-anchored LLM architecture that integrates biologically-inspired subsystems -- including HiPPO-initialized state-space models, zero-initialized...

item.title: Can LLMs Perceive Time? An Empirical Investigation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models cannot estimate how long their own tasks take. We investigate this limitation through four experiments across 68 tasks and four model families. Pre-task estimates overshoot actual duration by 4--7$\times$ ($p < 0.001$), with...

item.title: Finding and Reactivating Post-Trained LLMs' Hidden Safety Mechanisms
item.description: arXiv:2604.00012v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the impressive performance of general-purpose large language models (LLMs), they often require fine-tuning or post-training to excel at specific tasks. For instance, large reasoning models (LRMs), such as the DeepSeek-R1 series, demonstrate...

item.title: MSA-Thinker: Discrimination-Calibration Reasoning with Hint-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.00013v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis aims to understand human emotions by integrating textual, auditory, and visual modalities. Although Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved state-of-the-art performance via supervised fine-tuning (SFT),...

item.title: Voice conversations not shown in Claude.ai sidebar
item.description: Apr 2, 23:16 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Apr 2, 20:35 UTCUpdate - Although we are observing recovery once again, we expect continued periods of degradation. Work that is queued during times of degradation does eventually get processed. We continue to investigate and find a mitigation, and will update again within 2 hours.Apr 2, 19:28...

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A large-scale credential harvesting operation has been observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Services (AWS) secrets, shell command history, Stripe API keys, and GitHub tokens...

item.title: Elevated latency uploading Workers Static Assets
item.description: Apr 2, 18:47 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 2, 17:39 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating elevated latency uploading Workers Static Assets. Workers scripts which are already uploaded are not impacted.

item.title: Moonlake: Causal World Models should be Multimodal, Interactive, and Efficient — with Chris Manning and Fan-yun Sun
item.description: We cap out our World Models coverage with one of the most exciting new approaches - long running, multiplayer, interactive world models built with agents bootstrapped from game engines!

item.title: Unifying Group-Relative and Self-Distillation Policy Optimization via Sample Routing
item.description: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a standard paradigm for post-training large language models. While Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is widely adopted, its coarse credit assignment uniformly penalizes failed rollouts, lacking the token-level focus needed to...

item.title: Modular Energy Steering for Safe Text-to-Image Generation with Foundation Models
item.description: Controlling the behavior of text-to-image generative models is critical for safe and practical deployment. Existing safety approaches typically rely on model fine-tuning or curated datasets, which can degrade generation quality or limit scalability. We propose an inference-time steering framework...

item.title: Digital Experience Monitoring (DEX) Delay
item.description: Apr 2, 16:50 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue where Digital Experience Monitoring (DEX) data delivered via Logpush may experience delays. DEX dashboard functionality and other Logpush datasets are not affected.

item.title: ChatGPT Dictation Elevated Error Rate
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services and working on mitigating impact.Affected components Voice mode (Partial outage)

item.title: Copilot Coding Agent failing to start some jobs
item.description: Apr 2, 16:30 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Apr 2, 16:28 UTCUpdate - When assigning tasks to Copilot Cloud Agent, the task will appear...

item.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
item.description: Gemma 4: Our most intelligent open models to date, purpose-built for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

item.title: Lightweight Spatiotemporal Highway Lane Detection via 3D-ResNet and PINet with ROI-Aware Attention
item.description: This paper presents a lightweight, end-to-end highway lane detection architecture that jointly captures spatial and temporal information for robust performance in real-world driving scenarios. Building on the strengths of 3D convolutional neural networks and instance segmentation, we propose two...

item.title: Adam's Law: Textual Frequency Law on Large Language Models
item.description: While textual frequency has been validated as relevant to human cognition in reading speed, its relatedness to Large Language Models (LLMs) is seldom studied. We propose a novel research direction in terms of textual data frequency, which is an understudied topic, to the best of our knowledge. Our...

item.title: AstroConcepts: A Large-Scale Multi-Label Classification Corpus for Astrophysics
item.description: Scientific multi-label text classification suffers from extreme class imbalance, where specialized terminology exhibits severe power-law distributions that challenge standard classification approaches. Existing scientific corpora lack comprehensive controlled vocabularies, focusing instead on broad...

item.title: Cache Purging Delays
item.description: Apr 2, 12:58 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 2, 12:43 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.Apr 2, 12:41 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare single-file cache purging (purge by URL) called via the Dashboard/API are delayed. We are working to resolve this issue as soon...

item.title: Issues with creating Managed Rules exceptions via the Cloudflare Dashboard
item.description: Apr 2, 12:42 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with creating Managed Rules exceptions via the Cloudflare Dashboard. These issues do not affect the Cloudflare API, serving of cached files via the Cloudflare CDN or other security features at the Cloudflare Edge. Customers using...

item.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.  As the war in Iran continues, one of the most visible global economic ripple effects has been...

item.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
item.description: As the war in Iran continues to engulf the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, one of the most visible global economic ripple effects has been fossil-fuel prices. In particular, you can’t get away from news about the price of gasoline, which just topped an average of $4 a gallon in...

item.title: High error rate for completionsapi-gpt-5-nano
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Chat Completions (Degraded performance)
